text: Artificial intelligence is developing rapidly, and many experts predict that general AI—machines able to perform any intellectual task like a human—could arrive within the next few decades. This is sometimes called artificial general intelligence, or AGI. The potential benefits are huge: AGI could help solve complex problems like climate change, disease, and poverty. However, there are also serious risks. For example, an uncontrolled AGI might make decisions that harm people. Scientists disagree on the timeline. Some think AGI is only 10–20 years away, while others believe it will take much longer. Either way, careful planning is needed to ensure this technology is safe and beneficial for everyone.
